nodes.note: Live-voice ENTRY (PE-T3) in LINEUP mode. The SAME node as the support demo — only the settings differ (target is a channel, mode:'lineup'). A listener asking to speak surfaces as an utteranceFinal; the host has already queued them. $json carries { event, target, mode:'lineup', speakerId, audioFileId, ... }.

Live-voice ACTION (PE-T4). Opens the mic to this listener (explicit userId = jump them to the front; blank would pop the next in queue per the call's order). The granted user id is saved at $json.granted so the greeting can address them.

params.text: نوبت شما رسید. بفرمایید سؤالتان را بپرسید.

nodes.note: TTS leg (PB-T7 reused!). Synthesizes the moderator's 'it's your turn' prompt to audio and stores it as a CTB file id at $json.speech.fileId. No AI node was added for Phase E — the broadcast moderator reuses the same TTS node.
Live-voice ACTION (PE-T4). Plays the moderator prompt into the channel broadcast so every listener hears who has the mic. source:'file' → the host reads the stored bytes (I6).

Live-voice ACTION (PE-T4). Closes the current speaker's turn so the queue can advance — the next utteranceFinal re-enters this flow and grants the next person. Together grant→speak→endTurn implement a Q&A line entirely from generic nodes + settings (invariant I2).
